pending.body: finding_summary: `MilestoneControllerResumePort::task_status()` in both CLI runtime adapters still collapses `RunStatus::Failed` into `ControllerTaskStatus::Pending` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1265-1282`, `src/cli/run.rs:992-1007`), but the new resume state machine now depends on a distinct `Failed` status to enter failure reconciliation from `running`/`reconciling`/`blocked` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1172-1227`). Because `execute_resume()` accepts failed snapshots as resumable work (`src/cli/run.rs:3618-3633`), a crash between project failure and milestone sync can be resumed as a fresh retry without ever recording the failed attempt, which undercounts retries and can bypass `NeedsOperator` escalation. affected_files: src/cli/milestone.rs, src/cli/run.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs. remediation_hint: Return `ControllerTaskStatus::Failed` for failed run snapshots in both runtime adapters and add a regression test that a failed running project resumes into the failure-reconciliation path instead of `claimed`.

finding_summary: `resolve_controller_for_next()` now preserves `Blocked` controllers that carry retry context (`src/cli/milestone.rs:648-659`), but `inspect_next_milestone_action()` still treats every `Blocked` state as "no ready beads" and drops the bead payload (`src/cli/milestone.rs:563-609`). After a retryable failure, `milestone next` reports a generic blocked milestone instead of surfacing the failed bead that should be resumed, so operators and any tooling that relies on `next` lose visibility into the actionable retry work. affected_files: src/cli/milestone.rs. remediation_hint: Mirror the `blocked_retry_context` handling from `execute_milestone_run()` in `inspect_next_milestone_action()` and return the active bead as the next actionable item when `Blocked` includes bead/task ids.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 24 -> Plan and Implement
